name: Espinama - Fuente De by the Portilla del Boquejon – circular through the Picos de Europa National Park
name_original: Espinama - Fuente Dé por la Portilla del Boquejón – circular por el Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa
region_intro: Jogging routes in Lic-Rio Deva traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Deva and Nansa rivers, offering a mix of fluvial environments and dramatic gorges. The region is characterized by limestone mountains, deep valleys, and verdant pastures, particularly as it integrates with the Picos de Europa National Park. Runners can expect varied terrain, from river paths to more challenging ascents within a protected natural area. Best jogging routes in Lic-Rio Deva The most popular jogging route is Espinama - Fuente De by the Portilla del Boquejon – circular through the Picos de Europa National Park, a 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers expansive views within the Picos de Europa. Another top favourite among local runners is Nogalón loop from Potes, a difficult 4.2 miles (6.7 km) path. This running route features significant elevation gain, providing a challenging experience around Potes. Local runners also love the Fuente Dé – Fuente Dé Cable Car loop from Espinama, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 0 minutes. Jogging in Lic-Rio Deva is defined by its fluvial environments, dramatic gorges, and limestone mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate river paths to difficult mountain running routes. The routes in Lic-Rio Deva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Lic-Rio Deva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Lic-Rio Deva offers a selection of over a dozen running routes, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from easy river paths to more challenging mountain trails within this protected natural area.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there is at least one easy route available. The initial segments of the Nansa River Path are described as relatively easy and suitable for families, offering a picturesque itinerary along the riverbed. Overall, the region provides a mix of terrain, including moderate river paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The terrain in Lic-Rio Deva is quite varied. You can expect picturesque river paths along the Deva and Nansa rivers, some with wooden walkways and stairs carved into rock. Other routes venture into dramatic limestone mountains and deep valleys, offering more challenging ascents and descents, especially those integrating with the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999012599/espinama-fuente-de-by-the-portilla-del-boquejon-circular-through-the-picos-de-europa-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espinama - Fuente De by the Portilla del Boquejon – circular through the Picos de Europa National Park&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Dé – Fuente Dé Cable Car loop from Espinama&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through mountainous terrain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I might encounter on a run?
A: Running in Lic-Rio Deva offers stunning natural beauty. The Nansa River Path provides views of the riverbed and the opportunity to reach the 'Viewpoint of the Poet'. Trails in and around La Hermida Gorge offer dramatic vistas of imposing cliffs and the Deva River. Higher points, especially those connected to the Picos de Europa, provide expansive views of mountain ranges and valleys.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Yes, some sections of the Nansa River Path are considered easy and accessible, making them suitable for families. These paths offer a pleasant experience along the river, with varied terrain that can include wooden walkways and gentle ramps.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The running routes in Lic-Rio Deva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene river paths to dramatic gorges and challenging mountain ascents, all set within a rich and protected natural environment.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a challenging run?
A: Absolutely. Lic-Rio Deva, particularly with its proximity to the Picos de Europa National Park, offers several routes with significant elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20500704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogalón loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult path featuring substantial climbs, providing a challenging experience for trail runners.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions near the running routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. You can find attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/991425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Potes&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4135071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Infantado Tower&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Lebeña Church&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Infantado Tower – Historic center of Potes loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt;, even incorporate these landmarks directly into the running experience.

Q: Is it possible to combine running with a refreshing swim in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Yes, during warmer months, the Nansa River offers the option for a refreshing swim. Some sections of the Nansa River Path run alongside the river, making it convenient to cool off after your run amidst the picturesque natural surroundings.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The diverse landscapes of Lic-Rio Deva make it suitable for running across multiple seasons. While specific seasonal conditions can vary, the warmer months allow for refreshing swims in the river. The region's natural beauty, from verdant pastures to dramatic gorges, can be enjoyed throughout much of the year, though checking local weather and trail conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there any moderate running routes for those looking for a balanced challenge?
A: Certainly. Lic-Rio Deva offers several moderate running routes that provide a good balance of challenge and accessibility. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24634059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a satisfying experience without the extreme difficulty of some mountain trails.
tips: Here you have a view of the hundreds of meters high cliffs that lie like an amphitheater around Fuente Dé.

This place is famous for its impressive glacial cirque and the Fuente Dé Cable Car, which allows visitors to ascend to a height of 1,823 metres in just 4 minutes. 
Fuente Dé is also a starting point for various hiking and mountaineering routes, such as the ascent to Alto de la Triguera and the circuit around Peña Remoña. In addition, the area is rich in biodiversity and offers opportunities for observing flora and fauna.

waypoints.name: View of the Cirque of Fuente Dé
